results.title: What is phishing? (definition of phishing, with examples)
results.summary: Phishing is a fraudulent attempt, typically via email, to steal personal information such as credit card numbers, social security numbers, or passwords. These emails often impersonate well-known organizations and direct users to fake websites where they are prompted to enter sensitive information. To protect yourself, it's crucial to recognize phishing attempts by looking for signs such as poor-quality website visuals and suspicious URLs. Legitimate sites will use "https" for secure connections, while phishing sites may have forged URLs or start with an IP address. For more detailed information and examples, visit [PhishTank](https://phishtank.com/what_is_phishing.php?view=we).

## What is phishing? Phishing is a fraudulent attempt, usually made through email, to steal your personal information. The best way to protect yourself from phishing is to
learn how to recognize a phish. Phishing emails usually appear to come from a well-known organization and ask for your personal information — such as credit card number, social security number, account number or password. Often times phishing attempts appear to ... In order for Internet criminals to successfully "phish" your personal information, they must get you to go from an email to a website. Phishing emails will almost always tell you to click a link that takes you to a site where your personal information is requested. Legitimate organizations would never request this information of you via email.
** Show website annotations **
### Examples: Phishing email /Phishing website
 
*What to look for in a phishing website* 1. **Poor resolution.** Phishing websites are often poor in quality, since they ... 2. **Forged URL.** Even if a link has a name you recognize somewhere in it, it doesn't mean it links to the real organization. Read URLs from right to left — the real domain is at the end of the URL. Also, websites where it is safe to enter personal information begin with "https" — the "s" stands for secure. If you don't see "https" do not proceed. Look out for URLs that begin with an IP address, such as: http://12.34.56.78/firstgenericbank/account-update/ — these are likely phishes. results.title: How to Recognize a Scam Website | ScamAdviser
results.summary: The webpage titled "How to Recognize a Scam Website" on ScamAdviser provides essential tips for identifying fraudulent websites. Key indicators of a scam site include poor website design, lack of contact information, and suspicious payment methods. Users are advised to check for secure connections (HTTPS), read reviews, and look for trust seals. The article emphasizes the importance of researching the website's reputation and using tools like ScamAdviser to verify legitimacy. For anyone concerned about online safety, this guide serves as a practical resource to avoid scams.

January 21, 2019

results.title: Livehunt
results.summary: The Livehunt feature from VirusTotal allows users to continuously monitor and analyze files, URLs, and domains for potential threats in real-time. It provides automated alerts for newly detected malicious content, enabling proactive security measures. Users can customize their search parameters and receive notifications based on specific criteria. This tool is particularly useful for cybersecurity professionals looking to enhance their threat intelligence capabilities. For more details, visit the [Livehunt documentation](https://docs.virustotal.com/docs/livehunt).

The webpage provides a comprehensive guide to the .tel domain, a unique top-level domain that allows users to store and manage their contact information directly within the Domain Name System (DNS). Key features of .tel include:

1. **Contact Management**: Users can consolidate various contact details (phone numbers, email addresses, social media links) under a single .tel address, making it easier for others to reach them.

2. **Instant Updates**: Changes to contact information are updated in real-time, ensuring that friends and colleagues can always find the latest details.

3. **Customizable Privacy**: Users can control who sees their information, with options to encrypt data and share it selectively.

4. **Location Sharing**: The .tel domain allows users to share their current location, which can be useful for personalized services.

5. **Keyword Usage**: Users can add keywords to describe themselves, enhancing their online presence.

Overall, the .tel domain serves as a versatile tool for managing personal and professional contact information efficiently.

THE CONCEPT The.tel is a new top-level domain that allows the.tel domain owner to store, publish and manage contact information and keywords directly in the Domain Name System (DNS), the global and powerful system that stores locations of all the websites in the world. This is the first service of its kind that enables anyone to use the DNS to store information other than the location of websites. It provides an exciting new use of internet infrastructure that will completely change the way we communicate and keep in touch with others.
The.tel allows you to: ... • Manage contact information, text, keywords and location • Create folders that allow easy navigation, like london.uk.hertz.tel
• Create Profiles, such as work, home, travelling ... Wherever life takes you, you’ll always be in the right place for your friends, family, colleagues and associates to find you. Your.tel domain name is accessible from any ... WHAT CAN I DO?
Take a moment to familiarize yourself with your new.tel domain name. Let’s remind you of ... It’s one simple contact address for life – All you need to do is to give your friends, family and colleagues your.tel address, and they will then be able to keep in touch with you how 
and when you want them to. All of your contact information is in one place – The.tel enables you to store all of your ... social media pages, your blog sites, location-based sites, links to OpenID, OpenSocial or ... all under one simple name. You can also store your information in an encrypted form, so no ... Tell the world about yourself – Your.tel also allows you to use an unlimited number of ... you can access it from any device with an internet connection through a browser. You don’t ... significant benefit of not storing the information as HTML on a website – the.tel is fast, lowcost and easy to access in any format. With.tel, you do not need to host, manage and ... the.tel enables you to be discovered more quickly through search engines. The structured information, keywords and links to a domain name all enable search engines to see the ... Easily change your published profile – You will be able to set up a profile like ‘work’, ‘home’, ‘travel’, ‘holiday’ and so forth (the profile titles will only be visible to you) that quickly ... depending on which profile you make active.

WHAT SHOULD I AVOID USING MY.TEL FOR? Keyword functionality should only be used to help people find you if you haven’t given them your.tel domain name. It is not the right place for you to store your CV or information you might easily forget. Make sure that you only put information that is helpful for people to find ... When you register your.tel domain name, you are provided with a username and password to manage your.tel domain name, as well as a link to the management panel where you can store, change, update and publish contact information under your.tel. Use the username and password to log into the.tel control panel. Never give anyone else your username and/or password for your.tel. This is crucial in maintaining the security and integrity of your contact information. Telnic will never ask you ... Once you have logged into the control panel, you have complete control over your.tel. The information you store as private will not be accessible by anyone without your permission. ... THE DASHBOARD
Once you’ve registered your.tel domain, you will be routed to the management console or Dashboard (see Fig.1). This is the main control panel for populating your new.tel domain with contact information, text, keywords and, if you would like, a location.
